title: Fermented Jamaican Hash
description: A hearty, plant-based friendly stew featuring fermented ingredients, crispy textures and a rich red wine flavor, perfect for a sick day.
instructions: Combine the red wine and vegetable broth in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the carrots, sweet potatoes, red onion, scotch bonnet peppers, allspice, thyme, and salt to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Using a slotted spoon, transfer the vegetables to a bowl and let cool to room temperature.
Strain the cooking liquid through a fine mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Return the cooking liquid to the pot and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce the liquid by half, stirring occasionally, until it has reached a thick, syrupy consistency.
Stir in the chopped cilantro and cook for 1-2 minutes, or until the mixture is heated through.
Transfer the hash to a serving dish and serve warm or at room temperature.
Garnish with additional cilantro, if desired.
